questions.name: ES modules
questions.question_text: What does export do in an ES module?

answers.text: Makes code available to other modules
It provides a modern development workflow with quick feedback
answers.html: It provides a modern development workflow with quick feedback

answers.text: It removes the need for JavaScript

questions.name: Vue component
questions.question_text: A Vue component is best thought of as:

answers.text: A reusable, state-aware interface building block

questions.name: Reactive state
questions.question_text: When reactive state changes, Vue can update the view automatically.

questions.name: Props
questions.question_text: A child Vue component receives count as a prop and tries to run props.count++ when a button is clicked. What is the better pattern?

answers.text: Emit an event or call a parent-provided handler so the parent updates the prop value
answers.html: Emit an event or call a parent-provided handler so the parent updates the prop value

answers.text: Mutate the prop directly because props are writable local state
answers.html: Mutate the prop directly because props are writable local state

answers.text: Store the new count in localStorage so the parent sees it
answers.html: Store the new count in localStorage so the parent sees it

answers.text: Query the parent DOM and overwrite the rendered text directly
answers.html: Query the parent DOM and overwrite the rendered text directly

questions.name: ref-dot-value
questions.question_text: In a Vue <script setup> component, a developer declares const count = ref(0) but writes count = count + 1 in a click handler. What is the problem?

answers.text: ref values must be read and updated through .value — the correct expression is count.value = count.value + 1
answers.html: ref values must be read and updated through .value — the correct expression is count.value = count.value + 1

answers.text: ref() is not available inside script setup
answers.html: ref() is not available inside script setup

answers.text: count must be declared with let, not const
answers.html: count must be declared with let, not const

answers.text: The click handler must use an arrow function for ref to work
answers.html: The click handler must use an arrow function for ref to work

questions.name: named-export-import
questions.question_text: A file exports export function formatPrice(n) { ... }. Which import statement correctly brings it into another file?
